items.title: Samsung’s Best OLED TV Is $300 Off
items.summary: The gorgeous Samsung S95F QD-OLED is on super sale ahead of the newest models hitting stores.

items.title: FDA Approves Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 Pill
items.summary: Eli Lilly’s once-daily Foundayo is the second obesity pill to receive FDA approval. It will compete with Novo Nordisk’s pill version of Wegovy.

items.title: AI Models Lie, Cheat, and Steal to Protect Other Models From Being Deleted
items.summary: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.

items.title: De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
items.summary: Blockchain trackers put the cryptocurrency heist in the hundreds of millions of dollars and is already on track to be the largest crypto theft in 2026 so far.

items.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
items.summary: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

items.title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
items.summary: Delve faces new allegations that it violated the open source license of its customer, Sim.ai, by taking the customers's tool and passing it off as its own.

items.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
items.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

items.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
items.summary: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

items.title: Families cram into Greek court for trial into deadliest train crash
items.summary: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

items.title: Visa launches new AI tools to manage the charge dispute process
items.summary: Visa's new tools are part of a larger push by major banks and financial institutions to incorporate AI into their businesses.

items.title: Warren Buffett says Iran bomb would make nuclear disaster harder to avoid
items.summary: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman said the growing number of nuclear-armed states has fundamentally altered the global risk landscape.

items.title: Warren Buffett says he sold Apple too soon and would buy more of it, though not in this market
items.summary: Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway's largest holding even after the conglomerate trimmed its stake at the end of last year.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: NATO’s Rutte schedules Washington trip as Trump reevaluates alliance
items.summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte will meet with President Trump in Washington next week as Trump publicly weighs pulling the U.S. out of the alliance over the war in Iran.  A White House official confirmed the development to The Hill after The Wall Street Journal first reported on Rutte’s visit. NATO spokeswoman Allison Hart told…

items.title: Iranian president leaves door open to diplomacy in open letter to US
items.summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian released a letter addressed to “the people of the United States of America” on Wednesday, hours before President Trump is scheduled to address the nation about the U.S. military’s ongoing operations against Iran.  “Today, the world stands at a crossroads,” Pezeshkian wrote in a letter posted on the social platform X. …

items.title: Millions start work too early. This drug helps them stay awake
items.summary: Millions of people start work before sunrise—but their brains aren’t ready for it. A new clinical trial has found that the wake-promoting drug solriamfetol can significantly boost alertness in early-morning shift workers struggling with shift work disorder. Participants who took the drug were able to stay awake and function better throughout full shifts, with improvements in productivity, safety, and daily performance.

items.title: A 200-year-old light trick just transformed quantum encryption
items.summary: Scientists have unveiled a new approach to ultra-secure communication that could make quantum encryption simpler and more efficient than ever before. By harnessing a 19th-century optics phenomenon called the Talbot effect, researchers developed a system that sends information using multiple states of single photons instead of just two, dramatically boosting data capacity. Even more impressive, the setup works with standard components and requires only a single detector, reducing cost and complexity.

items.title: Scientists found a baby dinosaur hidden in rock and it is surprisingly cute
items.summary: Scientists uncovered a rare baby dinosaur in South Korea and named it Doolysaurus after a famous cartoon character. Using cutting-edge CT scans, they discovered hidden bones—including a skull—inside rock much faster than traditional methods. The young dinosaur, possibly fluffy and lamb-like, even had stomach stones that reveal it ate a mix of plants and small animals. The discovery suggests many more dinosaurs may still be hidden in Korea’s rocks.

items.title: Artemis II sends humans around the moon for first time since Apollo
items.summary: Artemis II is underway, carrying four astronauts around the moon on a 10-day mission to test systems for later lunar flights and an eventual moon base.

items.title: Digital heart twins can guide a lifesaving procedure
items.summary: Heart replicas helped doctors spot good targets for ablation in 10 patients. Months later, all of them are free of sustained faulty rhythms.

items.title: A fossil reveals early relatives of spiders — armed with claws
items.summary: A Utah fossil shows early relatives of spiders and scorpions already had distinctive front claws 500 million years ago.
